Title: Isolation and characterization of mutants corresponding to the MENA, MENB, MENC and MENE enzymatic steps of 5′-monohydroxyphylloquinone biosynthesis in Chlamydomonas reinhardtii

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: late prenylation step of phylloquinone pathway
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers a phytyl side chain to 1,4-dihydroxy-2-naphthoate to form demethylphylloquinone.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of MENA abolishes OH-PhQ accumulation, reduces PSI content and photosynthetic electron transfer, causes slow growth in low light, high-light sensitivity, and prevents reactivation of photosynthesis after dark anoxia acclimation.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. The AS2 insertion mutant was mapped to MENA and identified as a phylloquinone-biosynthesis mutant. 2. mena extracts lacked detectable OH-PhQ by UPLC-MS. 3. mena showed reduced PSI abundance and lower PSII relative electron transfer rate in low light, severe PSI photoinhibition in high light, and defective photosynthetic reactivation after dark anoxia. 4. Exogenous phylloquinone did not rescue mena growth, suggesting a special requirement for MENA during vitamin K1 assimilation or side-chain replacement.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Slow growth under low light; severe high-light sensitivity; lack of phototrophic growth under high light; impaired recovery of photosynthesis after anoxia.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: low light; high light; dark anoxia acclimation; exogenous phylloquinone supplementation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: insertional mutagenesis; co-segregation/linkage analysis; TAIL-PCR mapping; UPLC-MS metabolite profiling; growth assays; ECS spectroscopy; chlorophyll fluorescence analysis
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Unlike other men mutants, mena was not rescued by exogenous phylloquinone; authors hypothesize MENA may also be required for assimilation/repair of exogenous PhQ side chain.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: 4A+ derived insertion mutant AS1 (menb); complemented strain menbR
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of MENB abolishes OH-PhQ accumulation and causes defective PSI function, lower electron transfer, slow growth in low light, high-light sensitivity, and loss of photosynthetic reactivation after dark anoxia; complementation restores wild-type behavior.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. The AS1 insertion mutant was mapped to MENB. 2. menb lacked OH-PhQ, whereas the complemented strain menbR restored wild-type fluorescence behavior and OH-PhQ peak. 3. menb displayed lower PSI content, increased PSI antenna size, reduced rETRII, and near-complete PSI inactivation after high-light exposure. 4. menb failed to reactivate photosynthesis after dark anoxia, and this defect was rescued in menbR.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Slow growth; high-light sensitivity; impaired phototrophic growth under stress light; anoxia-related photosynthetic defects.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: low light; high light; dark anoxia acclimation; exogenous phylloquinone supplementation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: insertional mutagenesis; co-segregation analysis; TAIL-PCR mapping; gene complementation; UPLC-MS metabolite profiling; ECS spectroscopy; chlorophyll fluorescence induction; growth assays
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: MENB carries a predicted PTS2 and is proposed to function in the peroxisomal part of the pathway.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts SHCHC into o-succinylbenzoate within the PHYLLO multi-enzyme route.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of the MENC-related PHYLLO function abolishes OH-PhQ accumulation, decreases PSI content and electron transfer, increases high-light susceptibility, and blocks photosynthetic recovery after dark anoxia.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: PHYLLO tetramodular men-homologous enzyme; MenC domain
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. The AO1 insertion mutant was mapped to the PHYLLO locus and classified as menc. 2. menc lacked detectable OH-PhQ. 3. menc showed reduced PSI abundance, reduced rETRII, and strong PSI photoinhibition after high light. 4. The study supports a PHYLLO-based early pathway in Chlamydomonas analogous to plant systems.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Slow growth and severe high-light sensitivity with impaired phototrophic growth.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: low light; high light; dark anoxia acclimation; exogenous phylloquinone supplementation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: insertional mutagenesis; co-segregation analysis; TAIL-PCR mapping; UPLC-MS metabolite profiling; chlorophyll fluorescence analysis; ECS spectroscopy; growth assays
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Authors note that disruption of PHYLLO may affect the whole fused MenF/MenD/MenH/MenC multi-enzyme, so menc may represent impairment of broader PHYLLO function rather than only MenC catalytic activity.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Paromomycin-derived insertion mutant 25.1 (mene); complemented strain meneR
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: peroxisomal activation step of naphthoquinone branch
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Activates o-succinylbenzoate by ligation to coenzyme A, forming o-succinylbenzoyl-CoA.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of MENE abolishes OH-PhQ accumulation and causes impaired PSI-dependent photosynthesis, slow growth under low light, high-light sensitivity, and failure to reactivate photosynthesis after dark anoxia; complementation restores the phenotype.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Mutant 25.1 was mapped to MENE. 2. mene lacked OH-PhQ, while complementation in meneR restored wild-type fluorescence and OH-PhQ accumulation. 3. mene had reduced PSI content, increased PSI antenna size, depressed rETRII, and very strong PSI photoinhibition in high light. 4. The protein carries a predicted PTS1 and is proposed to act in a peroxisomal segment of the pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Slow growth; high-light sensitivity; impaired phototrophic growth; defective photosynthetic recovery after dark anoxia.
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: peroxisome (predicted from PTS1 motif SRL); possible similarity to dual-targeted plant counterpart discussed
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: low light; high light; dark anoxia acclimation; exogenous phylloquinone supplementation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: insertional mutagenesis; co-segregation analysis; TAIL-PCR mapping; gene complementation; UPLC-MS metabolite profiling; chlorophyll fluorescence analysis; ECS spectroscopy; growth assays
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Authors infer peroxisomal localization from a C-terminal PTS1 motif; direct localization was not experimentally tested in this paper.
